task: Fix all client-side state management for favorites, subscriptions, and notifications: Stage 1 — extract FavoritesContext, SubscriptionsContext, and CompareContext as React Context providers in src/context/, wire them into src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx, remove all favorites-update/subscriptions-update/compare-update event buses. Stage 2 — add write protection to every mutation: favorites toggle, subscribe/unsubscribe, notification mark-read, submission cancel/withdraw — rollback on failure, toast, pending Set. Stage 3 — fix NotificationsBell tab-focus re-fetch: add syncedForUser ref.

stages.title: Fix all client-side state management
stages.spec:
STAGE 1 — Extract context providers (NO behavior change, NO UI change)

## Why this will be good
Current state is held inside hooks (useFavorites, useCompare, useProjectSubscriptions) that each maintain their own copy and sync via window custom events (favorites-update, subscriptions-update, compare-update). Every consumer re-fetches on mount and the event bus is brittle (NotificationsBell piggy-backs on subscriptions-update to re-fetch /api/notifications — not what the event name implies). Moving to React Context gives: one source of truth per domain, zero stale reads, no event bus, no re-fetch storm. Stage 1 MUST be invisible to users (same data, same API shape) so REVIEW and QA can verify zero regression.

Production-ready:
- Zero call-site changes for consumers that already use the hook names (`useFavorites`, `useCompare`, `useProjectSubscriptions`) — preserve the exact return shapes.
- SSR safety — providers are client components; server components continue to render fine because the app already wraps everything in `src/app/providers.tsx` (client boundary).
- No flash of empty state on mount — initial state logic (localStorage read, DB fetch) must happen exactly as it does today.

## Files to create

1. `src/context/FavoritesContext.tsx` — new file.
   - Export `FavoritesProvider` (client component, `"use client"`).
   - Export `useFavorites()` consumer hook that reads the context.
   - Move ALL logic currently in `src/components/PropertyActions.tsx` lines 24-147 (FAV_KEY, useFavorites body) into the Provider. The Provider holds `ids` state and exposes the same return shape: `{ ids, toggle, clear, has }`.
   - Preserve EXACTLY:
     - Initial load logic: `status === "loading"` guard, `synced` ref behavior, DB+localStorage merge on login, localStorage fallback on unauth.
     - `toggle`: optimistic update for both authed (DB write) and anon (localStorage write). Fire-and-forget fetch with `.catch(() => {})`. Stage 1 must NOT add rollback — that's Stage 2.
     - `clear`: same as today.
   - REMOVE the `window.addEventListener("favorites-update", ...)` listener and all `window.dispatchEvent(new ... "favorites-update" ...)` calls — the Provider's single `setIds` is now the fan-out mechanism. Context re-renders all consumers automatically.
   - Throw a clear error from `useFavorites()` if called outside the provider: `throw new Error("useFavorites must be used within FavoritesProvider")`.

2. `src/context/CompareContext.tsx` — new file.
   - Export `CompareProvider` and `useCompare()`.
   - Move `useCompare` logic from `src/components/PropertyActions.tsx` lines 149-195 (CMP_KEY, state, toggle, clear) into the Provider.
   - Preserve return shape: `{ ids, toggle, clear, has }` and the 3-item cap logic in `toggle`.
   - Remove the `compare-update` event listener and all `window.dispatchEvent(new Event("compare-update"))` calls.
   - Keep localStorage read on mount and localStorage write on toggle/clear (this is the persistence layer — not being removed, only the event bus).

3. `src/context/SubscriptionsContext.tsx` — new file.
   - Export `SubscriptionsProvider` and `useProjectSubscriptions()`.
   - Move logic from `src/hooks/useProjectSubscriptions.ts` into the Provider.
   - Preserve return shape EXACTLY: `{ subscribedSlugs: string[], loading: boolean, refresh: () => void }` (the `UseProjectSubscriptionsResult` interface).
   - Drop the module-level `cachedPromise` / `cachedForAuthed` dedup — with a single Provider there is exactly one fetch per session state change, so module-level dedup is no longer needed. If you prefer to keep it, it must live inside the provider's useEffect, not at module scope.
   - Remove the `window.addEventListener("subscriptions-update", ...)` listener. Expose `refresh()` as the mutation-side API (which SubscribeButton and ProfileClient will call directly — wired in Stage 2, but expose it now).

## Files to modify

4. `src/components/PropertyActions.tsx`
   - Delete the `useFavorites` function (lines 24-147) and the `useCompare` function (lines 149-195).
   - Re-export from the new context files so existing import paths keep working:
     ```ts
     export { useFavorites } from "@/context/FavoritesContext";
     export { useCompare } from "@/context/CompareContext";
     ```
   - Keep everything else in the file (SharePopover, CardActions, DetailActions) untouched.

5. `src/hooks/useProjectSubscriptions.ts`
   - Replace file contents with a thin re-export:
     ```ts
     export { useProjectSubscriptions } from "@/context/SubscriptionsContext";
     export type { UseProjectSubscriptionsResult } from "@/context/SubscriptionsContext";
     ```
   - Move the `UseProjectSubscriptionsResult` interface into `SubscriptionsContext.tsx` and export it from there.

6. `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`
   - Import `FavoritesProvider`, `CompareProvider`, `SubscriptionsProvider`.
   - Wrap `{children}` inside `<Providers>` with all three providers. Order: `FavoritesProvider > CompareProvider > SubscriptionsProvider > children + FavoritesDrawer`. (Order doesn't functionally matter — pick one and keep it stable.)
   - Providers must sit INSIDE the existing `Providers` (SessionProvider) wrapper because they call `useSession()`.

7. `src/components/NotificationsBell.tsx`
   - Remove the `useEffect` that listens for `subscriptions-update` (lines 79-84).
   - Import `useProjectSubscriptions` from `@/hooks/useProjectSubscriptions`.
   - Subscribe to the context's `subscribedSlugs` — when it changes, call `loadNotifications()`. Pattern:
     ```ts
     const { subscribedSlugs } = useProjectSubscriptions();
     useEffect(() => { if (status !== "loading") loadNotifications(); }, [subscribedSlugs, status, loadNotifications]);
     ```
   - DO NOT also keep the existing `useEffect([status, loadNotifications])` — merge them into one that depends on `[status, subscribedSlugs, loadNotifications]`. Guard with `if (status === "loading") return;`.

8. `src/components/SubscribeButton.tsx`
   - Delete the `dispatchSubUpdate` helper and every `dispatchSubUpdate()` call.
   - Import `useProjectSubscriptions` (same path as today), pull `refresh` from it, and call `refresh()` at every place that currently calls `dispatchSubUpdate()`: after `subscribeAuthed` success, after `unsubscribeAuthed` success (inside try), after `subscribeAnon` success.

9. `src/components/ProjectSubscribeSection.tsx`
   - Delete the `dispatchSubUpdate` helper (line 46-48) and its call sites.
   - Replace with `refresh()` from `useProjectSubscriptions()`.

10. `src/app/[locale]/profile/ProfileClient.tsx`
    - In `doUnsubscribe` (line 237-251), remove the `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("subscriptions-update"))` line. Keep `refreshSubs()` — it already does the same job via the context.

## Zero-change rule for other consumers
These files import `useFavorites` / `useCompare` / `useProjectSubscriptions` and MUST NOT be edited (they work automatically via the re-exports):
- `src/components/SavedPropertiesSection.tsx`
- `src/components/FavoritesDrawer.tsx`
- `src/components/Navbar.tsx`
- `src/components/PropertyComparison.tsx`

If you touch any of these files in Stage 1, the stage has scope-crept. Revert.

## Acceptance criteria
- `grep -rn "favorites-update\|subscriptions-update\|compare-update" src/` returns ZERO matches.
- `grep -rn "window.dispatchEvent" src/` — the only remaining matches should be outside the 3 domains above (e.g. unrelated UI events). Zero matches for `favorites-update`, `subscriptions-update`, `compare-update`.
- `tsc --noEmit` passes (run `npx tsc --noEmit` or `npm run build`).
- App boots, favorites heart toggles, subscribe button toggles, compare works — all visually identical to before. No console errors. No "useFavorites must be used within FavoritesProvider" errors anywhere in the tree.
- Login flow: log in while having anon favorites in localStorage — they must merge into DB exactly as today.
- NotificationsBell still updates when a new subscription is created (via the context dependency replacing the event listener).

## Commit
Single commit at end of stage: `refactor: extract Favorites/Compare/Subscriptions into React Context providers, remove event-bus fan-out`

## Out of scope for Stage 1 (do NOT do these — they are Stage 2 or 3)
- Rollback on fetch failure.
- `pending` Set / `isPending` API.
- Toast on error.
- NotificationsBell tab-focus `syncedForUser` ref (Stage 3).
- Any UI change, styling, or new component.


STAGE 2 — Write protection on every mutation (rollback + toast + pending)

## Why this will be good
Today every mutation is fire-and-forget with optimistic UI. When the network fails, the UI stays in the optimistic state and the server-side truth silently diverges. A user favorites a property, sees the heart, closes the tab, and the DB never recorded it. Stage 2 makes every mutation honest: on failure we revert the UI and tell the user with a toast. We also add an in-flight `pending` set so users can't double-click their way into a bad state.

Production-ready:
- Rollback is the SAME state value captured before the optimistic setter, not a re-computed guess.
- Pending state is per-id (not a single global boolean) so one slow favorite doesn't lock the whole drawer.
- Toast uses the existing `useToast` hook from `@/hooks/use-toast` (same one used elsewhere — no new dependency).
- No change to success paths: if the server returns ok, behavior is identical to Stage 1.

## The rollback pattern (use this EXACTLY for every mutation below)
```ts
const prev = ids;  // capture BEFORE optimistic update — MUST be from the snapshot, not functional setter
const nextIds = removing ? prev.filter(x => x !== id) : [...prev, id];
setIds(nextIds);
setPending(s => { const n = new Set(s); n.add(id); return n; });
try {
  const res = await fetch(url, { method, ... });
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
} catch {
  setIds(prev);  // revert to exact previous state
  toast({ title: "Couldn't save favorite", description: "Please try again.", variant: "destructive" });
} finally {
  setPending(s => { const n = new Set(s); n.delete(id); return n; });
}
```

Critical: capture `prev` from the snapshot (`const prev = ids`) — NOT from inside a functional `setIds(prev => ...)` callback. Functional setters run during render-batching; we need the pre-mutation value at call time to revert to.

## Changes by file

### 1. `src/context/FavoritesContext.tsx`
- Add `pending` state: `const [pending, setPending] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set())`.
- Expose `isPending(id: string): boolean` on the context: `isPending: (id) => pending.has(id)`.
- `toggle(id)`:
  - Authed path: capture `prev = ids`, set optimistic, add to pending, `await fetch(...)`, on `!res.ok` OR catch revert with `setIds(prev)` + toast, finally remove from pending.
  - Anon path: capture `prev = ids` AND `prevLocal = localStorage.getItem(FAV_KEY)`. Write optimistic to state + localStorage. On error (rare for localStorage but possible quota issues) revert both. Pending Set still applies so UI can disable the button during the microtask.
- `clear()`:
  - Authed: capture `prev = ids`, set `[]`, `await Promise.all(...)`. On any rejection, `setIds(prev)` + toast ("Couldn't clear favorites"). No per-id pending — use a boolean `clearing` flag or just block clear button while any pending exists.
- Export `useFavorites()` return shape: `{ ids, toggle, clear, has, isPending }`.

### 2. `src/context/SubscriptionsContext.tsx`
- Add `pending: Set<string>` of slugs and `isPending(slug)`.
- Provide `subscribe(slug, { projectName, projectImage, email? })` and `unsubscribe(slug)` methods on the context (new — today SubscribeButton does its own fetch). These methods own: optimistic update of `subscribedSlugs`, pending set, fetch, rollback, toast.
  - Exposed context shape becomes: `{ subscribedSlugs, loading, refresh, subscribe, unsubscribe, isPending }`.
- Capture `prevSlugs = subscribedSlugs` before mutation. On failure `setSubscribedSlugs(prevSlugs)` + toast.
- Success toast for subscribe stays where it is today (SubscribeButton shows its own local toast on success — keep that). The context-level toast is ONLY for errors.
- Anon subscribe path still writes to `binayah_project_subscriptions` localStorage; revert on failure as with favorites.

### 3. `src/components/SubscribeButton.tsx`
- Replace the direct fetch logic in `subscribeAuthed`, `unsubscribeAuthed`, `subscribeAnon` with calls to `subscribe()` / `unsubscribe()` from the context. The local state `subscribed`, `loading`, and the success toast stay (they're UI, not state-of-truth).
- Wire `loading` to `isPending(slug)` so multiple buttons for the same slug on the same page all disable together.
- Remove `setSubscribed(true)` / `setSubscribed(false)` optimistic lines in this file — subscription status now derives from `subscribedSlugs.includes(slug)` (already the useEffect on line 98-100). The context handles optimistic.

### 4. `src/components/NotificationsBell.tsx`
- `markRead(item)`:
  - Capture `prev = items` before the optimistic `setItems((p) => p.map(...))`.
  - Switch to `await` pattern: on `!res.ok` or catch, `setItems(prev)` + toast("Couldn't mark as read"). On success, proceed with `setOpen(false)` and `router.push(...)`.
  - Important: only revert IF authed (localStorage writes don't fail in a way we need to surface).
  - Keep the navigation behavior — revert the read state if the mark-read call failed BEFORE navigating. If user already navigated away, toast won't show; that's acceptable.

### 5. `src/app/[locale]/profile/ProfileClient.tsx`
- `doCancel(id)`: capture `prev = submissions`, optimistically set status to "cancelled", await. On `!res.ok` or catch: `setSubmissions(prev)` + toast("Couldn't cancel submission"). Currently the code only mutates state inside `if (res.ok)` — flip it: set optimistically BEFORE the fetch, revert on failure. This is the observable behavior change QA tests for.
- `saveEditSub()`: same pattern — capture `prev = submissions`, apply edit optimistically, await, revert on failure + toast("Couldn't save changes").
- `doUnsubscribe(slug)`: delegate to context `unsubscribe(slug)` instead of the direct fetch — the context handles rollback + toast.
- `toggleNotif(slug, channel)`: capture `prev = profileExtra`, apply optimistic, await, revert on failure + toast("Couldn't save notification preference"). Today it's fire-and-forget.

## Toast wiring
- Import `useToast` from `@/hooks/use-toast` in each provider and in the files above. Inside the provider: `const { toast } = useToast()`.
- Variant: `"destructive"` for all rollback toasts.
- Message template: title 3-5 words ("Couldn't save favorite"), description one short sentence ("Please try again.").
- One toast per failed action — no spam if a user smashes the button (pending Set prevents that).

## QA must test (tell QA in the QA spec)
- Favorite heart click with `/api/favorites` forced to 500 (devtools Fetch override OR block URL): heart reverts, toast appears.
- Subscribe with `/api/project-subscriptions` forced to fail: button reverts to "Subscribe", toast appears.
- Notification bell item click with `/api/notifications` PATCH forced to fail: unread dot returns, toast appears, no navigation.
- Profile "Cancel submission" with `/api/list-your-property` PATCH forced to fail: status stays as it was, toast appears.
- Rapid double-click on favorite heart: second click ignored because `isPending(id)` is true (button disabled or click no-ops).

## Acceptance criteria
- Every mutation listed above uses `await fetch` and checks `res.ok` before treating the call as success.
- Every mutation has a rollback branch that restores the exact pre-mutation state.
- Every mutation calls `toast({ variant: "destructive", title, description })` on failure.
- `isPending` is exposed from FavoritesContext and SubscriptionsContext and is used to disable at least the primary button in at least one call site (SubscribeButton). Buttons MAY ignore `isPending` for clicks that can safely queue (favorites heart may or may not; pick one and be consistent).
- No new UI elements. No styling changes. Toast is the existing app-wide toaster.

## Commit
Single commit at end of stage: `feat: add write-failure rollback + pending state + error toasts to favorites/subs/notifications/submissions mutations`

## Out of scope
- Any retry logic (no exponential backoff, no auto-retry).
- Offline queue.
- Stage 3 (NotificationsBell re-fetch fix).


STAGE 3 — NotificationsBell: stop re-fetching on tab focus

## Why this will be good
After Stage 1, `loadNotifications` is called from a `useEffect` that depends on `[status, subscribedSlugs, loadNotifications]`. `subscribedSlugs` is a new array reference every time the context provider re-runs (which happens on session changes), and even `loadNotifications` as a `useCallback` can change if its deps change. This effect will fire more often than needed. The user-visible symptom: every time you switch back to the tab, a network request to `/api/notifications` goes out. For authed users with many notifications that's wasteful; for the bell badge it's a flash of "loading" then back to the count.

The fix: a `syncedForUser` ref that records "I already synced for user X" and short-circuits re-runs. Same pattern that `useFavorites` already uses (PropertyActions.tsx line 31, `synced` ref) and that worked.

## Change (single file)

`src/components/NotificationsBell.tsx`

1. Import `useRef` (already imported).
2. Inside the component, BEFORE `loadNotifications`:
   ```ts
   const syncedForUser = useRef<string | null>(null);
   ```
3. Modify `loadNotifications` — inside the `useCallback`:
   - At the top, AFTER deciding `isAuthed`:
     - If `isAuthed` and `syncedForUser.current === session?.user?.id`, return early (we already have the data).
     - If NOT `isAuthed` and `syncedForUser.current === null`, the anon localStorage path runs once on first load.
   - Simpler unified guard:
     ```ts
     const currentKey = isAuthed ? session!.user!.id! : "__anon__";
     if (syncedForUser.current === currentKey) return;
     ```
   - After a successful fetch (authed) or successful localStorage read (anon), set `syncedForUser.current = currentKey`.
   - If the fetch throws or returns non-ok, do NOT set `syncedForUser.current` — next invocation should retry.
4. Handle logout / session change:
   - When `isAuthed` becomes false AND it was previously a user id, reset the ref:
     - Simplest: the guard above already handles it — `currentKey` becomes `"__anon__"`, which doesn't equal the previous user id, so the effect re-runs once and then sets ref to `"__anon__"`.
   - No separate useEffect needed for this.
5. Add an explicit `refresh()` callable that can be invoked from `subscribedSlugs` changes (Stage 1 wired this) — but the syncedForUser guard would block it. Fix: when `subscribedSlugs` changes AND user is authed, we WANT to re-fetch (a new subscription can create a new notification). So:
   - Split the cache key: `syncedForUser` stays keyed on `userId`.
   - Add a second effect that calls `loadNotifications` with a bypass flag when `subscribedSlugs` changes:
     ```ts
     const forceLoad = useCallback(async () => {
       syncedForUser.current = null;  // invalidate
       await loadNotifications();
     }, [loadNotifications]);
     useEffect(() => {
       if (status === "loading") return;
       // On first mount, initial load covers us.
       // On subsequent subscribedSlugs change, force refresh.
       if (syncedForUser.current !== null) forceLoad();
     }, [subscribedSlugs, status, forceLoad]);
     ```

## Acceptance criteria
- Open DevTools Network, filter `/api/notifications`. Switch to another tab, switch back. NO new request fires.
- Subscribe to a project — ONE new request to `/api/notifications` fires (subscribedSlugs changed → forceLoad runs).
- Log out then log in as a different user — ONE request fires for the new user (syncedForUser ref no longer matches).
- Bell badge count stays stable during tab-focus cycles.
- No infinite loops. Dev tools Network tab is quiet.

## Commit
Single commit: `fix(NotificationsBell): skip duplicate /api/notifications fetch on tab-focus using syncedForUser ref`

## Out of scope
- Any other file. Stage 3 is a single-file fix.
- Any polling or realtime subscription (SSE, WS).

- `src/components/FavoritesDrawer.tsx`

If you touch any of these files in Stage 1, the stage has scope-crept. Revert.

- `tsc --noEmit` passes (run `npx tsc --noEmit` or `npm run build`).

- Login flow: log in while having anon favorites in localStorage — they must merge into DB exactly as today.

## Commit
Single commit at end of stage: `refactor: extract Favorites/Compare/Subscriptions into React Context providers, remove event-bus fan-out`

## Out of scope for Stage 1
- Rollback on fetch failure (Stage 2).
- `pending` Set / `isPending` API (Stage 2).
- Toast on error (Stage 2).
- NotificationsBell tab-focus `syncedForUser` ref (Stage 3).
- Any UI change, styling, or new component.
